'Playing such a shot is mind-boggling': Ashwin tears into Pant
description: Rishabh Pant is a celebrated match-winner in Indian Test cricket, known for his aggressive style. However, his tendency to play high-risk shots, particularly in the 80s and 90s, has led to frustrating dismissals. Ravichandran Ashwin believes Pant, with over 50 Tests, needs to prioritize game awareness and convert promising scores into centuries for greater team rewards.

description: A web developer's joke about a ₹370 biryani date at a comedy show led to his job loss after viral backlash. His employer fired him, citing company values and workplace impact, but also sparked debate on accountability versus second chances for the 22-year-old.

Hardik Pandya to miss Afghanistan ODIs due to low-grade quadricep sprain
description: Hardik Pandya will miss the upcoming ODI series against Afghanistan due to a mild leg sprain sustained during training. The all-rounder, who was at peak fitness, will remain in Bengaluru for at least two weeks for recovery under the guidance of medical staff. This setback prevents him from joining the squad as planned.

title: Bill Gates warns Microsoft, Amazon, Google on data center push
description: Bill Gates has told the AI industry it does not have permission to drive up household electricity bills, warning Amazon, Google, Meta and Microsoft that the old utility-funded grid model is finished. Speaking on CNBC, the Microsoft co-founder said hyperscalers must now pick data centre sites where the economics and politics hold. With 48 projects worth $156 billion already blocked in 2025 and public opposition at record highs, the buildout is colliding with sentiment.

Telangana student jumps into river to save 3 friends in US, gets trapped in fishing net, dies
description: A Telangana youth, 23-year-old Koduru Anuroop Reddy, tragically lost his life in the US while heroically rescuing three friends from drowning. His body returned home Tuesday for a funeral marked by deep emotion. Anuroop's bravery in risking his own life to save others has been lauded by the Chief Minister, highlighting his profound humanity.

Madhya Pradesh orders scrapping of 2-child rule for govt job
description: Madhya Pradesh's Chief Minister, Mohan Yadav, has taken a notable step by abolishing an unpopular draft rule that disqualified candidates with more than two children from government employment. This seemingly outdated regulation, in effect since 2001, had previously branded having more than two children as a misconduct issue for established staff.

Russian crude here to stay? Why India-US energy ties may be more about LPG, LNG than oil
description: Geopolitical developments in the last few months have dictated India’s energy supplies. Be it crude oil, LPG or LNG, India is largely dependent on the world to meet its needs, and the US-Iran war has disrupted supplies via the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Fear of Recurrence: How immune memory helps cancer survivors face their worst nightmare
description: As we observe National Cancer Survivors Month, we delve into an exciting new era in the battle against cancer recurrence: the remarkable memory of our immune systems. Research indicates that our bodies can recognize and target cancer cells long after traditional treatment has concluded.

title: More than butter chicken: Why India can't stop craving Punjabi food
description: Punjabi cuisine, a cornerstone of Indian comfort food, has endured global culinary shifts by embodying emotion, hospitality, and home. From ancient roots to post-Partition dhabas and modern restaurants, its rich flavors and hearty simplicity continue to resonate, offering a taste of tradition and belonging that nourishes the soul.

title: Yamal, Endrick, Guler & Co: The young guns ready to storm FIFA World Cup 2026
description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will have 48 nations, 104 matches and a thousand storylines, but nothing like the arrival of a new generation of players announcing themselves on the world's biggest stage. These are the players who watched the 2022 Qatar finals as schoolboys and are now walking into dressing rooms alongside Messi, Ronaldo and Mbappé. The expanded format means more room for young talent to showcase their game. Here are 10 under-21 players stepping onto the World Cup stage for the very first time.
